summary: Matched the common fighter escape/dodge translation unit by replacing most of the relevant ft_08A4 assembly with a new C source file, wiring it into the build, and tightening related fighter headers/types needed by Samus, Yoshi, and shared collision/escape logic. The PR report shows src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Escape.c at 100.00% for the file and all listed functions.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Escape.c with matched C implementations for escape-related functions including ft_8009917C, ftCo_80099264, ftCo_800992A8, ftCo_80099314, ftCo_80099390, ftCo_80099438, ftCo_Escape_Anim, ftCo_Escape_IASA, ftCo_Escape_Phys, ftCo_Escape_Coll, ftCo_80099754, ft_80099794, ftCo_8009980C, ftCo_80099894, ftCo_800998EC, and ftCo_80099954.
Removed approximately 625 lines from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, including the matched escape functions and local sdata2 constants .L_804D8678/.L_804D867C.
Added ftCo_Escape.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit contributes to the text object list while the remaining ft_08A4 assembly still builds for unmatched contents.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Escape.h and moved/declared escape prototypes there; removed ft_8009917C and ft_80099794 declarations from ft_0877.h and ftCo_8009980C from ft_08A4.h.
Added common escape motion variables to ftCommon_MotionVars as mv.co.escape.x0 and mv.co.escape.x4, corresponding to offsets fp+2340 and fp+2344.
Expanded ftCommonData fields around offsets 0x314 through 0x324, exposing escape input thresholds/timers used by the matched code: x314, x318, x31C, x320, and x324.

Changed Samus special attributes height_attributes from a raw float[6] to ftCollisionBox and adjusted scale loops in Samus and Pikachu attr loading to iterate using sizeof(height_attributes) / sizeof(float).

Typed previously unknown helper prototypes used by the new C code, including ft_800847D0(ftCo_GObj*, ftCollisionBox*), ft_80085004(ftCo_GObj*), ftCo_800DF8B0(ftCo_Fighter*), ftCo_800DF8E8(ftCo_Fighter*), and ftYs_Init_8012BDA0(ftCo_GObj*).
Added ftCo_Escape.h includes to common guard/wait and several character files that reference escape functions.
Removed the SCALE_HEIGHT_ATTRS macro from ft/inlines.h and replaced its two shown uses with local explicit loops.
Added an ASM macro in placeholder.h and updated tools/calcprogress/src/find_asm_functions.py so calcprogress recognizes both asm and ASM declarations.
smart_moves: Kept character-specific behavior separated inside dispatcher functions: ftCo_800992A8 branches to Samus, Yoshi, or default escape setup, and ftCo_80099894 branches to Yoshi or default neutral escape setup.
Modeled the reused escape state at fp+2340/fp+2344 as a named ftCo_EscapeVars struct inside the common motion-vars union instead of continuing raw offset use.
Used small static inline helpers for repeated input-threshold checks, making the stick X and stick Y escape gate logic more readable while still matching.
Handled special character callbacks explicitly: Samus escape installs custom Anim and Coll callbacks, while Yoshi escape calls Yoshi init/guard helpers and installs a custom Anim callback.
Preserved matching-only stack-padding evidence with MUST_MATCH guarded unused stack arrays and @todo comments rather than hiding unexplained stack artifacts.
Converted height attribute typing toward ftCollisionBox and used sizeof-based loops, reducing dependence on a magic attribute count while preserving the scaling behavior.
Updated calcprogress to recognize the new ASM macro, preventing macro-wrapped assembly declarations from being missed by tooling.
decomp_lessons: When a matched C file replaces only part of a large assembly file, add the new C object before the remaining asm object and remove only the matched assembly ranges/constants.
Escape code depends on common input timing fields in ftCommonData; exposing only the needed offsets can unblock matching without fully naming the entire common-data tail.
Character-specific action states often require callback wiring through fp->cb fields, not just a Fighter_ChangeMotionState call.
Motion variable offsets like fp+2340/fp+2344 should be represented in the appropriate per-action union member once their action ownership is known.
For matching code that has duplicate control flow, small local inlines can encode repeated conditions while retaining the compiler shape.
Do not leave shared prototypes as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS when matched callers reveal concrete signatures; tightening signatures helps future files include the right headers and avoid casts.
If a macro is used to conditionally expose assembly syntax, progress tools must be updated to parse that macro form or progress accounting can regress.
Use explicit @todo comments for suspected shared code and unused stack padding so future cleanup has a breadcrumb without changing the match.
naming_conventions: Common action files follow ftCo_ActionName naming: ftCo_Escape.c and ftCo_Escape.h.
Externally known common action callbacks use names like ftCo_Escape_Anim, ftCo_Escape_IASA, ftCo_Escape_Phys, and ftCo_Escape_Coll.
Still-unknown internal/static-like functions retain address-based names such as ftCo_800992A8, ftCo_80099314, ftCo_80099564, and ftCo_800998EC.
Motion IDs use ftCo_MS_EscapeF, ftCo_MS_EscapeB, and ftCo_MS_EscapeN for forward, back, and neutral escape.
Common-data and motion-var fields remain offset-style when semantics are not fully known, e.g. p_ftCommonData->x31C, x320, x324 and fp->mv.co.escape.x0/x4.
Headers use address comments in prototypes, e.g. /* 09917C */ bool ft_8009917C(ftCo_GObj* gobj);
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed matched assembly from ft_08A4.s while leaving the rest of the assembly translation unit in place.
Used MUST_MATCH conditional unused stack arrays in functions such as ftCo_80099390, ftCo_80099438, ftCo_80099564, and ftCo_80099954 to account for stack shape.
Represented bit-flag behavior through named fields/helpers such as fp->x221D_b5 and ftCheckThrowB3(fp), matching the assembly's byte/bit operations at fp+0x221D and fp+0x2210.
Matched action changes with Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0) followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4(gobj), mirroring the assembly sequence.
Preserved callback pointer assignments for special cases, including fp->cb.x21A0_callback_Anim and fp->cb.x21A8_callback_Coll.
Used sizeof(height_attributes) / sizeof(float) loops after changing height_attributes to ftCollisionBox, avoiding hard-coded SCALE_HEIGHT_ATTRS(6).
Added ASM macro support and updated the regex in find_asm_functions.py to match either asm or ASM, keeping assembly-function discovery compatible with conditional macro use.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt is an automated-style match report showing ftCo_Escape.c and all listed functions at 100.00%.
searchable_terms: ftCommonData x314 x318 x31C x320 x324
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_800992A8 or other address-based ftCo_Escape helpers to semantic names.
Search for follow-up cleanup of the MUST_MATCH unused stack arrays in ftCo_Escape.c.
Check later changes to ftCommonData fields x314, x318, x31C, x320, and x324 for semantic names.
Find later PRs that finish typing ftCollisionBox height_attributes across other character attributes.
Search for additional uses or removal of the ASM macro added in placeholder.h.
